summary: Matched the Target Test Falco stage module `grtfalco` by replacing `asm/melee/gr/grtfalco.s` with C, wiring `src/melee/gr/grtfalco.c.o` into `obj_files.mk`, adding a local header, and tightening a couple of related prototypes to use `enum_t`. The PR resolves #603 and had no substantive review comments beyond approval.

key_files.role: Removed 348-line assembly implementation after matching in C.
New matched C implementation for grtfalco stage callbacks, StageData, object setup, and item/line query helpers.
New declarations for grtfalco functions and callbacks.
Switched object list from asm/melee/gr/grtfalco.s.o to src/melee/gr/grtfalco.c.o.
Changed StageData callback5 parameter type from int to enum_t.
Changed func_80056B6C prototype from s32(s32) to enum_t(enum_t).

what_changed: Deleted the hand-written assembly file `asm/melee/gr/grtfalco.s` and added a 195-line C translation in `src/melee/gr/grtfalco.c`.
Defined the stage callback table `lbl_803E8918` as `StageCallbacks lbl_803E8918[4]`, preserving three populated entries and their flags, including `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)` for the third callback set.
Defined `StageData lbl_803E8974` for stage id `45`, callback pointers, asset path `"/GrTFc.dat"`, and flags value `1`.

Added initialization logic in `lbl_802207F4`: stores the result of `func_801C49F8()` in `lbl_804D6AF8`, sets `stage_info.unk8C.b4 = false` and `stage_info.unk8C.b5 = true`, spawns ids 0, 1, and 2 via `func_8022089C`, then calls common stage setup functions `func_801C39C0`, `func_801C3BB4`, `func_801C4210`, and `func_801C42AC`.

Implemented `func_8022089C(int id)` as the common GObj creation/setup helper: fetches the GObj with `func_801C14D0`, clears `Ground` callbacks at `x8_callback` and `xC_callback`, installs `GObj_SetupGXLink(gobj, func_801C5DB0, 3, 0)`, installs callback3 into `gp->x1C_callback`, invokes callback0, optionally schedules callback2 with `func_8038FD54`, and reports failure with `OSReport`.
Implemented simple callback stubs that return false, return true, or do nothing where the original assembly only returned constants or `blr`.
Used existing helpers such as `HSD_GObjGetUserData`, `Ground_JObjInline1`, `func_801C8138`, `func_801C2FE0`, and `func_800115F4` to express repeated ground-stage behavior.

Implemented `lbl_80220ACC(enum_t arg0)` as a collision/material-style query: reject `-1`, call `func_80056B6C`, require result `0`, call `func_80054C6C`, then select one of four fields from `lbl_804D6AF8` for bit values `1`, `2`, `4`, or `8`.
Added `grtfalco.h` with prototypes for all exported labels/functions used by the callback table and StageData.
Updated `StageData.callback5` and `func_80056B6C` prototypes to use `enum_t`, matching the new C usage in `lbl_80220ACC`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` so the build uses `src/melee/gr/grtfalco.c.o` instead of the deleted assembly object.
smart_moves: Modeled the assembly data section as typed C data (`StageCallbacks` and `StageData`) instead of raw `.4byte` values, making stage structure reuse clearer for later stage matches.
Kept unknown symbols with original `lbl_`/`func_` names where no semantic name was proven, avoiding over-naming while still enabling an asm-to-C match.
Introduced a small local `grTFalco_UnkStruct` with four `s32` fields for the sbss pointer at `lbl_804D6AF8`, enough to type the observed accesses without inventing field meanings.
Used bit-shift expressions such as `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)` and `(1 << 0)` through `(1 << 3)` rather than opaque hex constants, improving readability while preserving values.
Recognized repeated ground callback bodies and expressed them through existing inline/helper abstractions like `Ground_JObjInline1(gobj)`, reducing fragile manual pointer code.
Adjusted shared prototypes (`StageData.callback5`, `func_80056B6C`) to `enum_t`, making the matched function type-correct rather than forcing casts or mismatched integer types.
Preserved exact OSReport string content, including the unusual `"couldn t"` spelling and source line number `201`, which is important for matching embedded string/data layout.
decomp_lessons: For stage modules, first translate the callback table and `StageData`; these often explain function signatures and the order of initialization callbacks.
When assembly shows a data table of four function pointers plus flags repeated per entry, it likely maps to `StageCallbacks`; using the existing struct can replace many raw `.4byte` entries cleanly.
The `StageData` callback signatures may need small shared header corrections when a matched module reveals that an argument is an `enum_t` rather than a plain `int`.
Do not rename unknown stage callbacks prematurely; preserving address-derived names is acceptable when semantics are not established and helps keep the match auditable.
Global sbss pointers can be typed with a minimal local unknown struct when only offsets are known; field names like `unk_0`, `unk_4`, `unk_8`, `unk_C` are evidence-grounded.
Constant-return and empty assembly callbacks should be translated directly into `return false`, `return true`, or empty C functions rather than over-engineered logic.

For GObj setup code, matching C often needs the exact order: fetch object, access `gobj->user_data`, clear callbacks, call `GObj_SetupGXLink`, conditionally install callbacks, then schedule callback processing.
String literals in assembly data, such as asset path `"/GrTFc.dat"`, error messages, and source filename strings, should be preserved exactly in C initializers or calls.
naming_conventions: New header guard used the project style `MELEE_GR_GRTFALCO_H`.
File naming follows existing stage target convention: `src/melee/gr/grtfalco.c` and `src/melee/gr/grtfalco.h`.
Unknown callbacks and globals remained address-based (`lbl_802207F4`, `lbl_803E8918`, `lbl_804D6AF8`) where no better name was evidenced.
Temporary or local typed unknown data used `grTFalco_UnkStruct` with fields `unk_0`, `unk_4`, `unk_8`, `unk_C`.
Callback arguments are generic when semantics are unknown (`arg0`, `arg1`, `arg2`), while obvious objects use `gobj`, `gp`, `cb`, and `id`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the assembly callback array at `lbl_803E8918` into `StageCallbacks` initializers, including NULL-equivalent `FLAGS_ZERO` entries and the high-bit flag value for the third entry.
Converted raw data bytes for `"/GrTFc.dat"`, `"grtfalco.c"`, and the OSReport format string into C string literals, preserving data dependencies.
Used struct bitfields for `stage_info.unk8C.b4` and `stage_info.unk8C.b5` to match the original `rlwimi` byte-bit manipulation.
Matched simple `blr` functions as empty functions and `li r3, 0/1; blr` as boolean false/true returns.
Matched the GObj null check with an `if (gobj != NULL) ... else OSReport(...)` shape, reflecting the original branch structure.
Expressed comparisons against `1`, `2`, `4`, and `8` as `(1 << 0)` through `(1 << 3)`, which preserves value while communicating bitmask intent.
Used `enum_t` for the parameter and intermediate variable in `lbl_80220ACC`, consistent with `func_80056B6C` and `func_80054C6C` style APIs.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the slice.
One review by r-burns approved the PR with an empty body.
PR body states `Resolves #603` and includes `Co-authored-by: jeffm <jeffmao99@berkeley.edu>`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `grtfalco` callbacks and `lbl_804D6AF8`.
Compare `grtfalco.c` with other target-test stage modules such as `grtfox`, `grtdonkey`, or `grticeclimber` for shared callback/table patterns.
Search for uses of `StageData.callback5` after its parameter type changed to `enum_t` to see whether other modules required similar fixes.
Look for subsequent cleanup of `grTFalco_UnkStruct` fields once the data returned by `func_801C49F8` was understood.
